what_it_does: It is a synthesis index that consolidates 11 staged face proposals (drawn from 160 source pages) into a single ledger of proposed augmentations to the generative design DNA, organized by face, ordered by leverage, with conflicts, open questions, and a concrete proof path for verifying the merged DNA by regenerating a real decision slide.
observations: Claims ~130 augmentations across the proposals, of which ~25 are genuinely new, plus 8 proposed new sections/faces and ~7 cross-face conflicts.
Divided into five sections: by-face ledger, leverage-ordered build list, conflicts to resolve, open questions for Tim, and a C1 proof path.
Build order is explicitly dependency-driven: tokens → grammar rules → molecules → layouts/sequence.
Items are tagged with build gates such as [buildable-now] or [blocked-on-Tim].
Proposes a new content-budget system in dna/budgets.json with 12 empirical slots plus two inferred decision-surface slots (option-label and option-implication).
Identifies interactive molecules (pill, tag, glass_panel, status_dot) as a major unminted category.
Introduces a depth-compression law with three operators (FUSE, REDUCE, EXTRACT) under sequence.json > depth_compression.
Lists unresolved cross-face conflicts including figure-gold value (#E3C421 vs #C8A800), octagon/hexagon core assignment, green/red semantics, dashed vs solid meaning, and future/opacity encoding.
The C1 proof path maps fields from content/decisions.json[#merge-sa-authorize] through a renderer slot map to specific DNA augmentations and budgets.
Uses evidence-class labels: Observed for record/renderer/augmentation facts, Verified only after re-rendering and visual pass.
standouts: The C1 proof path makes the merge testable by sight: it ties two inferred content budgets (option-label and option-implication) directly to a real 390×844 decision slide.
The content-budget system and computed type role-table are positioned as the central mechanism that turns ~40 collection role names into one resolver.
Interactive molecules are called out as the single biggest blind spot of the print-first harvest.
The depth-compression law is presented as resolving a prior conflict between two compression models via FUSE/REDUCE/EXTRACT operators.
conventions: Heavy use of structured Markdown sections, tables, and one-line augmentation syntax (name · status · target).
Status taxonomy: new, extends, conflicts, confirmed/ratify.
Target-file references follow the pattern dna/<face>.json or dna/<file>.json > path.
Evidence is classified as Observed or Verified.
Uses design-system vocabulary: tokens, grammar, molecules, organisms, faces, DNA, chroma, scrim, hatch, ghost, goldwash.
concerns: A large share of proposed work is gated on decisions from a single person (Tim), creating a bottleneck.
Two content budgets are inferred from analogs with no source sample, and the proof path risks circular validation if C1 itself becomes the only source.
The document simultaneously proposes many new files, new sections, and new rules, which may be difficult to merge atomically.
Several cross-face conflicts remain unresolved and span multiple faces, so one decision ripples widely.
The proof path depends on renderer behavior and manifest files not shown in this file.
notes: A dense, decision-ready merge plan that turns source-page observations into a gated build list and a slide-level verification contract.
questions: What is the exact definition of a 'face' in this repository, and where are the 11 source proposal files located?
What does the current live DNA look like for the referenced files (tokens.json, shapes.json, grammar.json, etc.)?
Who or what is 'Tim' in this context, and how are the cross-face decisions recorded?
What is the full schema of the C1 decision record beyond the fields excerpted?
How does the renderer currently map decision record fields to visual slots, and is that mapping itself part of the DNA?
What distinguishes a 'molecule' from an 'organism' in this taxonomy?
Are the counts (160 pages, ~130 augmentations, ~25 genuinely new) derived automatically or estimated by the author?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is a synthesis/build index and it performs that role, adding a conflict ledger and a proof path.
apparent_themes: Generative design DNA encoded as machine-readable JSON.
Ratification versus extension versus genuine gap-filling.
intention_signals: It is trying to give a single approver a small, high-leverage decision surface so that a large set of proposed DNA changes can be merged safely and then validated by regenerating a real slide.
novelty: It combines a conventional build index with a verification contract: the C1 proof path explicitly links inferred content budgets to a real rendered slide and defines Observed/Verified evidence classes for that test.
contribution: This file contributes a structured synthesis ledger, a leverage-ordered build list, a cross-face conflict map, an open-question inventory, and a concrete proof-path specification that a fused system would keep as the merge plan and validation contract for the proposed DNA augmentations.
summary_for_embedding: A build index that synthesizes 11 staged face proposals into ~130 proposed design-DNA augmentations, organized by face, ordered by leverage, with cross-face conflicts, open questions, and a C1 slide proof path for verifying merged changes by sight.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a decision-ready merge plan and verification contract for updating the generative design DNA from source-page proposals.
